name: Temporal Lobe
location: Inferior lateral cerebral cortex situated beneath the Sylvian fissure
description: Houses primary auditory cortex, language comprehension centers, and deep limbic structures critical for long-term memory encoding and emotional processing.
functions: Auditory perception and pitch processing (Heschl's Gyrus A1)
Visual object recognition along ventral STREAM ('What' pathway)
bloodSupply: Middle Cerebral Artery (MCA) & Posterior Cerebral Artery (PCA)
clinicalImportance: Seizure foci in mesial temporal lobe cause complex partial seizures; left Wernicke's lesions cause fluent aphasia.
researchNotes: Single-unit recordings in human medial temporal lobe reveal concept cells ('neuro-concept units').
interestingFacts: Contains the fusiform face area (FFA), specialized for human facial recognition.
